title: prunus pumila subsp. susquehanae
intro: Prunus pumila subsp. susquehanae, also known as Chokeberry, is a shrub native to eastern North America, characterized by its small white flowers and black, edible berries. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its high antioxidant and anti-inflammatory content. The health benefits of Chokeberry are attributed to its ability to help lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and support immune function. Chokeberry can be prepared as a tea, juice, or supplement, and is often used in herbal medicine to prevent and treat various health conditions.
